blockingIssues.description: Environment variables in McpServerConfig.env are never applied when spawning the server process. McpClient::connect() calls tokio::process::Command::new() and .args() but never calls .envs() or .env(). The env HashMap is parsed and stored but silently ignored at runtime.

LSP tool names and argument formats are incorrect. (1) LspRenameTool calls 'rename' but actual tool is 'rename_symbol'. (2) LspWorkspaceDiagnosticsTool calls non-existent 'workspace_diagnostics'. (3) LspDocumentSymbolsTool calls non-existent 'document_symbols'. (4) Argument format uses {textDocumentUri, position: {line, column}} but mcp-language-server expects {filePath, line, column} (1-indexed).

McpToolWrapper doesn't implement behavioral_hints() from the Tool trait. MCP tool annotations (readOnlyHint, destructiveHint, idempotentHint) are parsed and stored but not exposed via the Tool trait interface. Callers using the trait interface get default hints (read_only_hint: false, destructive_hint: false, idempotent_hint: true) instead of actual MCP annotations.

appliedUpdates.description: Added mcp-language-server and tree-sitter-server entries to services.yaml as runtime dependencies for MCP features (from round 2)
Created .factory/library/mcp-server-tools.md documenting actual tool names and argument formats for mcp-language-server and tree-sitter-server (from round 2)

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document MCP server tool verification procedure: Workers implementing MCP client features must verify actual tool names and argument formats by checking the MCP server's tools/list response or source code. Do not assume tool names based on LSP specification names.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mcp-lsp-integration reviewer identified that 'rename' should be 'rename_symbol', and 'workspace_diagnostics'/'document_symbols' tools don't exist in mcp-language-server

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on tokio::process::Command environment variable handling (.envs()/.env() methods) for MCP client process spawning. When spawning subprocesses that need environment variables, the .envs() method must be called on the Command.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mcp-server-config reviewer identified that env handling was missing from MCP client implementation - env vars were being parsed but never passed to the subprocess
